text: Standing here in this pristine, modern hallway, I sense expectation. The glass wall feels like a gentle barrier to both knowledge and experience, inviting me forward while reflecting my own image back—a moment of dual observation. There is a clinical, almost futuristic order to the space, punctuated by the vivid fire safety reel on the wall. The cool air and silence make me contemplative; I am on the threshold of discovery, separated by just a transparent layer from a room promising immersion. I feel a quiet anticipation—a readiness to cross from observer to participant.
